title: Simplified Usage List of Particles -up to Day 30
html:
Simplified Usage List of Particles (up to Day 30)

Particles with nouns similar to English prepositions

Particle

Usage + Example

が (ga)

subjects in topic-less sentences (Day 10) - このちかくに レストランが ありますか。


objects with adjectival and some exceptional verbs (Day 11) - リンさん、まんじゅうが すきですか。

so-called はが constructions (Day 15) - にほんは ぶっかが たかいですね。

を (o)

objects with transitive verbs (Day 4) - パンと たまごを たべます。

に (ni)

indicating a place in existential sentences (Day 10) - このちかくに レストランが ありますか。

time (Day 5) - わたしは 12じに ねます。

recipient (Day 13) - わたしは 12じに ねます。 

source with verbs whose meaning is to receive (Day 14) - ともだちに このペンを もらいました。 

purpose (Day 20) - デパートへ かいものに いきます。

expressing "per" (Day 26) - 1しゅうかんに 3かい このレストランで たべます。

goal (Day 23) - ここに すわっても いいですか。

へ (e)

destination (Day 6) - だれと きょうとへ いきますか。

Use of「へ」and 「に」 to express destination 

 「へ 」 and 「に」 are sometimes interchangeable, but often「に」is the only correct particle to use, so 「に」 will always be correct but「へ」 use is somewhat restricted. In this course we use「へ」whenever it is possible to indicate both are possible, we use 「に」 whenever it is the only particle that should be used.

「へ」 roughly corresponds to "towards", which means it is much less common or impossible to use when the movement of the subject or object noun to the destination is momentary such as 「はいります/すわります」or the completion of the movement is strongly presupposed such as 「のぼります/おきま す("to put")

で(de)

place where actions occur (Day 4) - としょかんで しゅくだいを します。

means (Day 6, 13) - じてんしゃで がっこうへ きます。

expressing "in some language" (Day 13) - にほんごで なんですか。

と(to)

person with whom subject noun does some action (Day 6) - ともだちと きょうとへ いきます

から(kara)

starting point (Day 5,17) - (テストは) 1じから 3じまでです。

まで(made)

ending point (Day 5,17) - (テストは) 1じから 3じまでです。

の (no)

N1 の N2, roughly corresponding to English 'of'. But the word order is opposite to English, that is, 「N1 のN2」 is 'N2 of N1' (Day 1,2) - とうきょうの ろせんず, とりにくの りょうり

の (no)

placed after nouns or predicates and functioning as a noun phrase (Day 2)  - このきょうかしょは わたしのです。

 は is used as a topic marker (Day 1) こちらは アンナさんです。

Not only subjects but other elements can appear as a topic. In this example, the object is used as a topic, with は instead of を (Day 25) - そのしょるいは　いりぐちに　おいてください。



C - Particles for coordination and addition

と(to)

roughly 'and' (forming an exhaustive list) (Day 4) - パンと たまごを たべます。

や(ya)

indicating two or more examples (Day 10) - コンビニや スーパーが ありますよ。

とか(toka)

indicating one or more examples (Day 23) - かきごおりとか　わたあめとかを　たべたいです

There are several important differences between 「や」 and 「とか」:

1) 「とか」 is a little bit more casual so it is more common to use except for informal conversations

2) 「とか」and「や」can be used more than once in a sentence. While when using「とか」, you must use 「とか」after each noun in the sentence, only one use of「や」is necessary and the others can be omitted. One more difference is that while 「とか」can be used only with one noun, 「や」requires at least two nouns.

 3) 「や」 can be used with 「など」("and so on") placed after the final noun like 「AやBなど」, while 「とか」 cannot, as 「とか」 is a more direct expression for indicating examples, while 「や」 implies other choices.

も(mo)

too, also (Day 1) - これも ろせんず ですか。

not any (with interrogative words) (Day 4) - わたしは なにも たべません。

D - Particles placed after predicates and functioning like English conjunctions

が(ga)

but or while (expressing contrast) (Day 8) - ちょっと むずかしいですが、おもしろいです。

から(kara)

causal (like since or because) (Day 12) -だいがくせいのとき べんきょうしましたから すこし わかります。

から(kara)

after (with Vて) (Day 29) - かいぎの しりょうを つくってから かえります。

Particles placed at the sentence-final positions

か(ka)

interrogation marker (Day 1) - せんもんは なんですか

ね(ne)

indicates that the speaker thinks that the information can be shared with the listener and the listener may share the same opinion/feeling (Day 9) - おおきい がっこう ですね

used to confirm what the other speaker said (Day 10) - じどうはんばいきの となりですね

よ(yo)

expressing a speaker's feeling that what he/she is saying is new information to the hearer (Day 10) - コンビニや スーパーが ありますよ。

Very Important Notice

In casual or colloquial style Japanese, the particles が, を, へ, and some usage of に (place, purpose, and goal in this table) are omitted, while in written language except informal e-mails or SNS messages, omission never occurs. These omissions occur frequently, especially immediately before predicates. The omission of は also happens but less frequent than the other listed type particles in the section called"Particles with nouns similar to English prepositions"

In order for you to get used to particles during our course, we typically don’t omit actually omissible particles. However, occasionally show examples where particles are omitted, we apologize for this slight inconsistency.

You can download this cheat sheet here
